column1: // Golden cases for qparser (Step 4: q named monadics). Format: <input><TAB><expected AST>.
// Lines beginning with // are comments; blank lines are ignored.
// Expected value @ABORT means the input must make the parser exit nonzero.
// These cases exercise the one q idea: monadic verbs are named keywords
// (flip, neg, count, ...), not demoted glyphs. A glyph is always dyadic.
// The parser's demotion block is gone; arity is read from the token.
// ---- named monadics: each keyword scans as a KV1 verb ----
// ---- glyphs are always dyadic: no demotion in monadic position ----
// In K, +1 demotes to (+:;1). In q, + is dyadic and there is no demotion,
// so +1 is a parse error: the user must write flip 1.
// ---- dyadic glyph use is unchanged: nve works as before ----
// ---- explicit glyph-colon forms stay infix/update heads ----
// ---- named monadic after a noun is NOT a dyadic infix ----
// A named monadic (KV1) has no dyadic form, so `n til x` is not til[n;x].
// It is a te: n applied to (til x). `f til 10` is f applied to (til 10),
// and `5 til 10` is 5 applied to (til 10) -- the KV1 heads its own te,
// then the whole thing is the right operand of the leading noun.
// (+/) parenthesizes to a noun; the derived verb +/ is KL (variadic), so
// (+/)til 10 is the derived verb applied to (til 10).
// A named monadic can also be the right operand of a dyadic glyph:
// ---- dyadic keywords: same nve machinery as glyph infix ----
select from t cross u where a=1
// ---- adverbs work on both glyphs and named monadics ----
// q:  +1  ->  (+;1)     [no demotion; dyadic glyph, one arg]
// The parser builds the shape; eval catches the arity mismatch.
// ---- ksql still works: where is both a clause keyword and a monadic verb ----
select a from t where d>0
select a,b by c from t where d>0
// ---- clause keywords are content inside their own clause (q verb, not name) ----
// ---- out-of-order clause keywords are still errors ----
select a from t where b by c
// ---- ordinary names still parse as nouns (not keywords) ----
// ---- dyadic keywords (lj, bin, ...) are KV2 verbs, not nouns ----
// ---- lambdas, parens, sequencing: unchanged from K ----
// ---- lambda parameter lists: 2-element KL without params, 3-element with -------
// ---- table literals: desugar to ! (dict) and flip (monadic +) ----
